label_reasons: Pinned Django i18n view contains user-influenced redirect flows in set_language that should be constrained to allowed hosts/schemes.

findings.title: CWE-601: Open redirect in set_language() via redirect()
findings.description: `set_language()` passes user-controlled `next_url` to `redirect()` at L56. An attacker can craft a URL that redirects victims to a malicious site for phishing or credential theft.
findings.suggestion: Validate redirect targets against an allowlist of safe paths/domains. Use `url_for()` for internal redirects. Check with `url_has_allowed_host_and_scheme(url, allowed_hosts)`.
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: response = HttpResponseRedirect(next_url) if next_url else HttpResponse(status=204)
AFTER:      from urllib.parse import urlparse
            parsed = urlparse(next_url)
            if parsed.netloc and parsed.netloc != request.host:
                abort(400)  # block external redirect
            response = HttpResponseRedirect(next_url) if next_url else HttpResponse(status=204)

rule.title: Python open redirect via redirect()
rule.summary: Flags `redirect()` calls that forward user-controlled URLs without allowlist validation.
rule.remediation: Use `url_for()` for internal redirects or allowlist external destinations explicitly.

findings.description: `get_paths()` passes `path` (dynamic/path-like input) to `os.path.join()` at L141 without sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)

findings.title: CWE-862: Django CBV `JavaScriptCatalog` exposes get with no auth mixin
findings.description: `JavaScriptCatalog` implements `get` but does not inherit an auth-enforcing mixin and has no `@method_decorator(login_required)` / permission decorator. Class-based view methods can be reached without authentication. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Add `LoginRequiredMixin` / `PermissionRequiredMixin`, or protect dispatch/methods with `@method_decorator(login_required, name='dispatch')`.

rule.title: Django class-based view missing auth mixin
rule.summary: Flags Django class-based views implementing get/post without LoginRequiredMixin, PermissionRequiredMixin, or an equivalent auth decorator.
rule.remediation: Add LoginRequiredMixin / PermissionRequiredMixin or protect dispatch with method_decorator(login_required).
known_limitations: Heuristics are strongest on common Flask/FastAPI and Express/Router-style patterns.

rule.remediation: Use generic error handlers in production; log details with `logger.exception()` server-side only.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in open_resource() via os.path.join()

findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.

rule.title: Python path join with unsanitized user input
rule.summary: Flags `os.path.join` or similar path composition with tainted user input.
rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

findings.title: Silent exception swallowing in close()

findings.suggestion: Log and re-raise: `logger.exception('Unexpected error'); raise`

BEFORE: except Exception:
AFTER:              logger.exception("Unexpected error")
                    raise

rule.title: Silent exception swallowing
rule.summary: Flags broad exception handlers that swallow failures with `pass` or `continue`.
rule.remediation: Log and re-raise unexpected errors or catch only specific exceptions you can safely handle.
